---
title: Pick tangrams
author: Alicia Chen
date: 2025-12-19
format:
  html:
    code-fold: false
---

We want to pick 12 tangrams from Ji et al. (2022) with high Shape Naming Divergence (SND). These are the tangrams that are likely more opaque

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
import json
import requests
from IPython.display import SVG, display, HTML

sns.set_context("talk", font_scale=1.2)
sns.set_style("white")
plt.rc('axes.spines', top=False, right=False)

snd_vals = requests.get("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lil-lab/kilogram/refs/heads/main/analysis/behavior/measures/measures_data/dense_SND.json").json()
```

Sort SND values from low to high

```{python}
snd_vals_sorted = sorted(snd_vals.items(), key=lambda x: x[1])
print(len(snd_vals_sorted))
```

Write a function that, given the key for tangram, retrieves and displays the SVG

```{python}
def show_tangram(key):
    """Fetch and display the SVG for a tangram given its key."""
    url = f"https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lil-lab/kilogram/main/dataset/tangrams-svg/{key}.svg"
    response = requests.get(url)
    response.raise_for_status()
    display(SVG(response.content))
    return SVG(response.content)
```

Show top 40 tangrams (highest SND) in a grid

```{python}
def show_tangram_grid(tangrams, cols=6):
    """Display tangrams in a grid with their names and SND values."""
    base_url = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lil-lab/kilogram/main/dataset/tangrams-svg"

    html = '<div style="display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat({}, 1fr); gap: 10px;">'.format(cols)

    for key, snd in tangrams:
        svg_url = f"{base_url}/{key}.svg"
        html += f'''
        <div style="text-align: center; padding: 10px; border: 1px solid #ddd;">
            <img src="{svg_url}" style="width: 100px; height: 100px;">
            <div style="font-size: 12px; margin-top: 5px;">{key}</div>
            <div style="font-size: 10px; color: #666;">SND: {snd:.3f}</div>
        </div>
        '''

    html += '</div>'
    display(HTML(html))

# Top 40 tangrams (highest SND values)
top_40 = snd_vals_sorted[-40:][::-1]  # Reverse to show highest first
show_tangram_grid(top_40, cols=6)
```

Convert selected tangrams to black silhouettes

```{python}
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
import re
import os

def make_tangram_black(key, save_dir="client/public/"):
    """Fetch a tangram SVG, convert to solid black silhouette, and normalize to square viewBox."""
    url = f"https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lil-lab/kilogram/main/dataset/tangrams-svg/{key}.svg"
    response = requests.get(url)
    response.raise_for_status()

    # Parse SVG
    ET.register_namespace('', 'http://www.w3.org/2000/svg')
    root = ET.fromstring(response.content)

    # Normalize viewBox to square (center the content)
    viewbox = root.get('viewBox')
    if viewbox:
        parts = viewbox.split()
        min_x, min_y, width, height = map(float, parts)
        max_dim = max(width, height)
        if width > height:
            # Content is wider than tall, add vertical padding
            new_min_y = min_y - (max_dim - height) / 2
            new_min_x = min_x
        else:
            # Content is taller than wide, add horizontal padding
            new_min_x = min_x - (max_dim - width) / 2
            new_min_y = min_y
        root.set('viewBox', f"{new_min_x} {new_min_y} {max_dim} {max_dim}")

    for elem in root.iter():
        if 'fill' in elem.attrib:
            elem.set('fill', 'black')
        if 'stroke' in elem.attrib:
            elem.set('stroke', 'black')
        tag = elem.tag.split('}')[-1]  # Remove namespace
        if tag in ('polygon', 'path', 'rect', 'circle', 'ellipse'):
            elem.set('stroke', 'black')
            elem.set('stroke-width', '1')
        if 'style' in elem.attrib:
            style = elem.get('style')
            style = re.sub(r'fill:\s*[^;]+', 'fill:black', style)
            style = re.sub(r'stroke:\s*[^;]+', 'stroke:black', style)
            elem.set('style', style)

    svg_str = ET.tostring(root, encoding='unicode')

    os.makedirs(save_dir, exist_ok=True)
    filepath = os.path.join(save_dir, f"tangram_{key}.svg")
    with open(filepath, 'w') as f:
        f.write(svg_str)

    # display(HTML(f"<p><strong>{key}</strong></p>"))
    # display(SVG(svg_str))

    return svg_str

selected_tangrams = [
    "page7-255",
    "page9-46",
    "page5-28",
    "page7-107",
    "page3-35",
    "page-B",
    "page3-121",
    "page4-157",
    "page7-26",
    "page5-64",
    "page6-149",
    "page3-193",
    "page5-63",
    "page9-7",
    "page5-142",
    "page4-15"
]

for tangram in selected_tangrams:
    make_tangram_black(tangram)
```

View the resulting 12 tangrams in a 3x4 grid

```{python}
import cairosvg
from PIL import Image
from io import BytesIO

def show_tangram_grid_plot(tangram_keys, save_dir="client/public/", cols=4):
    """Display tangrams in a grid with labels."""
    rows = (len(tangram_keys) + cols - 1) // cols
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(rows, cols, figsize=(cols * 3, rows * 3))
    axes = axes.flatten()

    for i, key in enumerate(tangram_keys):
        filepath = os.path.join(save_dir, f"tangram_{key}.svg")
        # Convert SVG to PNG using cairosvg
        png_data = cairosvg.svg2png(url=filepath)
        img = Image.open(BytesIO(png_data))
        axes[i].imshow(img)
        axes[i].set_title(key, fontsize=10)
        axes[i].axis('off')

    # Hide any unused subplots
    for j in range(len(tangram_keys), len(axes)):
        axes[j].axis('off')

    plt.subplots_adjust(wspace=0.3, hspace=0.5)
    plt.show()

show_tangram_grid_plot(selected_tangrams, cols=4)
```
